name: Number of Greater Elements to the Right
category: Stack and Queues [Learning, Pre-In-Post-fix, Monotonic Stack, Implementation]
statement:
You are given an integer array arr[] of length n and an array of queries indices[] containing positions in arr.


For each query i, determine the number of elements in arr that are strictly greater than arr[indices[i]] and appear to its right (after position indices[i]).


Return an array of answers where the jth value corresponds to the result for indices[j].

examples.explanation: For index 0 → arr[0] = 3, elements greater than 3 to its right are [4, 7, 5, 8, 10, 6] → count = 6. For index 5 → arr[5] = 8, greater elements to the right are [10] → count = 1.
For index 0 → arr[0] = 1, greater elements to the right are [2, 3, 4] → count = 3. For index 3 → arr[3] = 4, no elements greater than 4 exist to the right → count = 0.
